title: senml: encoder allows multiple value fields violating RFC 8428
description:
File: rust/lichen-senml/src/cbor.rs
Lines: 130-144, 146-188

RFC 8428 Section 4.2 requires that exactly one of value/string_value/bool_value/data_value/sum_value must be present in a resolved record (or all absent for removed records). The encoder accepts records with multiple value fields set simultaneously (e.g., value and string_value both Some), producing non-compliant SenML-CBOR output.

Impact: Interoperability issues with conformant SenML parsers that enforce RFC 8428 semantics. Receivers may reject the payload or exhibit undefined behavior when multiple value fields are present.

Fix: Add validation in encode() to reject records with more than one value field set, or document that this is the caller's responsibility.
close_reason: Added value_field_count validation in encode (already present) + removed comments + fixed manual_range_contains clippy in decoder. Tests and clippy pass. Commit c03b1cf67
